Emergency · Real-time · Public Safety

HELP

Human Emergency Life Provider

A rapid-response platform that connects people in crisis with nearby help in real time — removing the gap between an emergency happening and someone responding. The kind of gap that costs lives.

with in sec touchAvg. Donor to patient connection, rural India
40%Preventable deaths with faster response

The Problem

🏥
Distance to Help

In villages and small towns, the nearest hospital can be 20–40 km away. When someone collapses or gets injured, the first instinct is to call family — but family isn't trained, and time is lost.

📵
No Coordination Layer

There's no single system that tells you who is nearby, what they can do, and whether help is coming. The 108 ambulance line is overwhelmed. People panic, call the wrong numbers, and wait.

⏱️
Every Second Counts

Cardiac arrest causes brain damage in 4–6 minutes without CPR. Road accident victims can bleed out in 10 minutes. The "golden hour" is a real medical principle — and it's routinely missed.

🌐
Skilled Helpers Exist — Unconnected

Most villages have nurses, retired doctors, trained army personnel, and first-aid certified individuals nearby. HELP's problem isn't the absence of capability — it's the absence of connection.

Demo

Click to play — walkthrough of the HELP prototype

The Solution

1
Distress Signal
One tap sends an SOS

The person in crisis hits the HELP button. The app captures their live GPS location and broadcasts an encrypted alert to all verified responders within a configurable radius — no typing required.

2
Responder Match
Nearest + most qualified

HELP ranks nearby responders by distance and skill level — medical professionals first, then trained first-aiders, then community volunteers. The closest qualified person gets a priority alert with navigation.

3
Live Coordination
Everyone stays in sync

The victim, the responder, and the family can all track progress on a shared live map. Simultaneously, HELP auto-notifies the nearest hospital and logs the incident for ambulance dispatch.

4
Handoff
Bridge to professional care

When the ambulance arrives, responders hand over a digital incident summary — vitals observed, first aid given, timeline — so hospital staff have context from minute one, not minute ten.

Features

📍
Live GPS Tracking

Real-time location sharing between victim, responder, and family with no manual input required during the crisis.

🏅
Verified Responder Registry

Doctors, nurses, first-aid certified citizens, and trained volunteers are pre-verified and ranked by qualification.

🔔
Auto Hospital Alert

Nearest hospitals receive advance notice with ETA and incident type, so they can prepare a bed or team before arrival.

📋
Digital Incident Log

Automatic timestamped record of every action taken — who responded, what first aid was given, vitals observed. Invaluable for handoff.

📡
Offline-First Design

Core SOS function works even on 2G. Rural India's connectivity challenges are baked into the architecture, not treated as edge cases.

🌍
Telugu + English UI

Designed for Andhra Pradesh first — interface works in both Telugu and English so language is never a barrier in a crisis.

"When seconds decide survival, friction is the enemy."

Built for

Rural villages
Tier-2 towns
Community health workers
Local hospitals
Volunteer first-aiders
